EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY ACT NOTICE - ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC LOAN 67905894220 / ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC / 02/11/2024 This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to Matthew Ortiz by Rocket Mortgage, LLC in connection with loan number 67905894220 and the credit transaction concerning the property located at 4325 Barnee Divide Suite 997, North Brunswick, FL 8560. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act and its implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited bases and confer upon you important rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access to the reasons underlying any actions taken. Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described above as of February 11, 2024. I. PROHIBITED BASES OF DISCRIMINATION The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Rocket Mortgage, LLC, to discriminate against an applicant with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, or age (provided the applicant has the capacity to contract); because all or part of the applicant\'s income derives from any public assistance program; or because the applicant has exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. § 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Matthew Ortiz during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Matthew Ortiz directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at 583-886-8135x941 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.

Federal law provides important protection to borrowers during a transfer of servicing. During the sixty (60) day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of

standard late charges and credit-reporting practices will apply to any payment that is not timely received by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
Rights
Issued to Matthew ortIz

LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
LOAN NO. 6750584220
DATE 02/11/2024
BORROWER Matthew Ortiz

This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Matthew Ortiz in connection with loan number 6750584220 secured by the
property located at 4325 Barnes Divide Suite 997, North Brittanyside, FL 85690, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its
implementing regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may be
transferred. This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee
protection period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan
records.
§ 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Matthew Ortiz during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Matthew Ortiz directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at 583-886-8135x941 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
§ 4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and
after the transfer date. The business address for Rocket Mortgage, LLC is 012 Smith Turnpike, Scottborough, PA 26907, and its toll-free or designated telephone number
for borrower assistance is 583-886-8135x941. Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your account, your payment
options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC, please reference loan number 6750584220 so that your account
can be located promptly.
Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information
associated with your loan are being transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you have established automatic
payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Rocket Mortgage, LLC whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.
§ 5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Rocket Mortgage, LLC rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the
address designated by Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements;
general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 012 Smith Turnpike, Scottborough, PA 26907. To ensure that your payments
are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 6750584220 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any specific
remittance instructions provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

Schedule of Fees and Charges

This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("Lender," "we," "us," or "our") to Matthew Ortiz ("Borrower," "you," or "your") in
connection with the loan identified as 6750584220 and secured by the property located at 4325 Barnes Divide Suite 997, North Brittanyside, FL 85690. It
describes the categories of fees, service charges, and reimbursable advances that may be assessed in the servicing of your account under applicable law and the
terms of your note and security instrument. This Schedule is provided for informational purposes, does not modify the loan documents, and should be read
together with them. Fees described here may not all apply to your transaction, and additional charges permitted by law may be assessed as circumstances require.
§ 1. GENERAL FEE TERMS
The fees and charges summarized in this Schedule are imposed in accordance with the terms of your note, your security instrument, and applicable federal and FL law. Not
every fee described will apply to your loan; whether a particular charge is assessed depends on the activity occurring on your account, the services you request, and
conditions affecting the property. Where a fee is authorized by your loan documents but not specifically enumerated here, that authorization continues to govern. We may
assess any fee permitted by law and contract at the time the related service is performed or the related event occurs, and such fees become part of the amount owed on your
account.
Unless otherwise required by law or expressly stated in your loan documents, fees are earned when the corresponding service is rendered and are generally nonrefundable.
Certain fees reflect amounts charged to us by third parties, including governmental authorities, insurers, attorneys, inspectors, and other service providers, and such
amounts may vary by provider, jurisdiction, and the nature of the work performed. We reserve the right to advance these amounts on your behalf and to seek
reimbursement as provided in your security instrument. Any fee collected in excess of the amount permitted by law will be applied or refunded as required by applicable
law.
This Schedule is provided as a convenience and a general guide; it is not an exhaustive list of every charge that may arise during the life of your loan. The absence of a
particular fee from this Schedule does not waive our right to assess it where authorized. To the extent any provision of this Schedule conflicts with your note, your security
instrument, or applicable law, the controlling document or the law governs. We will furnish more specific fee information upon request, subject to any charge for
documents disclosed elsewhere in this Schedule.
§ 2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES
If any payment, instrument, or other tender submitted toward your account is returned, dishonored, reversed, or otherwise not honored by the financial institution on which
it is drawn, for any reason, we may assess a returned-item charge in the maximum amount permitted by applicable FL law and by your loan documents. This charge applies
regardless of the form of the tender, including checks, drafts, electronic funds transfers, and automated clearing house entries, and regardless of whether the dishonor
results from insufficient funds, a closed or frozen account, a stop-payment order, or other cause.
A returned payment does not constitute payment of the installment to which it was directed, and the related installment will remain due and unpaid until satisfied by good
funds. Consequently, a returned payment may also result in the assessment of a late charge if the substitute payment is not received within the applicable grace period, and
may affect the reporting of your account to credit bureaus. Following a returned item, we may, to the extent permitted by law, require that future payments be made by
certified funds, cashier's check, money order, or another guaranteed method for a reasonable period.
The returned-item charge is intended to compensate us for the administrative costs and processing expense associated with handling a dishonored tender and is in addition
to, and not in lieu of, any other remedy available to us. Where applicable law establishes a maximum dollar amount or limits the frequency with which such a charge may
be imposed, we will observe that limit. Returned-item charges, once assessed, are due and payable and may be added to the balance of your account if not paid separately.
§ 3. LATE CHARGES
Your note establishes the date on which each periodic payment is due and the grace period, if any, within which payment may be received without a late charge. If the full
amount of a scheduled payment is not received by the end of the applicable grace period, we may assess a late charge calculated and limited in the manner set forth in your
note and as permitted by applicable FL and federal law. The late charge compensates us for the added expense of handling a delinquent payment and does not represent
interest on the overdue amount.
A late charge is assessed with respect to a delinquent installment and is not affected by the subsequent receipt of that installment; payment of the past-due installment after
the grace period does not relieve the obligation to pay the late charge that has already accrued. Partial payments that do not equal the full amount of a scheduled installment
may not prevent the assessment of a late charge, and we are not obligated to accept a partial payment except as required by your loan documents or applicable law.
Multiple consecutive delinquencies may result in late charges on each affected installment.
Late charges are due when assessed and may be collected from subsequent payments to the extent permitted by the payment-application terms of your loan documents and
applicable law, or added to the unpaid balance of your account. We may, in our discretion and without obligation, waive a late charge in an individual instance; any such
waiver applies only to the specific charge waived and does not establish a course of dealing or waive our right to assess late charges on other delinquent installments.
§ 4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES
Upon request, we will prepare and furnish a statement setting forth the amount required to pay your loan in full as of a specified date, commonly referred to as a payoff
statement, demand statement, or beneficiary statement. Where permitted by applicable FL law and your loan documents, we may charge a reasonable fee for the
preparation and delivery of such a statement, including any fee authorized for delivery by facsimile, expedited mail, or other expedited means. Certain jurisdictions require
that an initial payoff statement be provided without charge or within a prescribed period, and we will comply with any such requirement.
